other_observers: Mark Peterson, Joe Roller, Glenn Walbeck, Kathy Mihm Dunning, Cole Wild, and a few others.; Steve Mlodinow found it in the p.m. on 10/08/2012  Also seen later by Joey Kellner on this date and by Steve Brown, Jeannie Mitchell, Aaron Shipe, Susan Craing and others on 10/09/2012
specific_location: Directly northeast of the Last Chance Rest Stop migrant trap.
description: Warbler by bill, size and foraging methods. Yellow-headed with diffuse light gray auricular patch surrounded on all sides by yellowish including above the base of the dark bill. Back was dull gray and wings were greenish gray with two whitish wingbars. Breast was dull whitish with hints of buff at throat and just barely hints of darker flank streaks but not usually visible. Throat was very light buff yellow but it could also appear just dull whitish. Tail was about the same color as the back and the legs were dark gray.Behaviors: Actively foraged in weeds and sunflowers, sometimes on the ground. It did not appear to be interested in the seeds but on probably searced for the insects on the plants and probably the insects on the ground under the plants. Often foraged below eye level but also seen high in the insect-eaten leaves of a Siberian elm. Active not staying still or more than a second or two.Call: call notes when it flew but I can't describe them other than to say they were unique and not like the YRWAs, NAWAs, and BTBW nearbyPlumage: 1st year female
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
